mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures the marginal strength of consumer spending; sustained positive momentum in this series directly lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS momentum fails to predict positive SPX 20-day returns with a walk-forward Rank IC above 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window signals deteriorating household income prospects and weakening consumption, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of an elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4 percent alongside decelerating industrial production (INDPRO) creates a restrictive financial environment where credit-sensitive sectors contract simultaneously, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and INDPRO fails to produce a walk-forward Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal performs worse than either single-proxy model in isolation.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production (INDPRO) during monetary tightening cycles force rapid downward revisions to manufacturing earnings forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions do not produce negative SPX 5-day returns in at least 55% of tightening-cycle instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1.5 million annualized units signals weakening construction activity and durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as cyclical earnings estimates are cut.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 1500 are followed by positive SPX 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates improving factory utilization and operating leverage, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as near-term earnings surprises shift upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month acceleration is followed by negative SPX 5-day returns in more than 55% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirms that labor income is translating directly into consumption, amplifying the positive effect on corporate top-line growth and generating outsized positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal from PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to outperform the better of the two single-proxy signals by at least 2 percentage points annualized over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 4.5 percent, the restrictive cost of capital dominates equity valuation dynamics by elevating discount rates and dampening leveraged share buybacks, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% is followed by positive SPX 60-day returns in more than 50% of months over any 3-year rolling window.
